text.tspan: FINPAY API — PHASE 10 ENGINEERING CHALLENGES
Pipeline, Docker, and deployment problems encountered and resolved
CI failed — prisma migrate dev requires interactive input in non-interactive shell
fix: use migrate deploy in CI
prisma migrate dev prompts for a migration name when it detects schema drift. GitHub Actions has no TTY — the prompt hangs and the job times out
after the default 6-hour limit.
Fix: CI uses prisma migrate deploy which applies existing migrations without prompting. migrate dev is only for local development.
migrate deploy is the production and CI command.
App container could not connect to PostgreSQL — wrong port in DATABASE_URL
fix: internal Docker networking uses 5432 not 5433
The VM uses 5433:5432 to avoid conflict with system PostgreSQL. The 5433 is the HOST port. Inside Docker Compose, the app service
resolves postgres by container name and uses the INTERNAL port 5432.
Fix: docker-compose.yml app service DATABASE_URL uses postgres:5432 (internal). Local .env still uses localhost:5433 (external).
Both are correct — different networking contexts.
CI health check step ran before the server finished starting — connection refused
fix: poll loop with 15 × 2s retries
node src/server.js & starts the server in the background. The next CI step runs immediately — before Prisma has connected, before Redis is ready,
before the HTTP port is open.
Fix: a shell loop polls curl -sf /health for up to 30 seconds before the health check step runs. If the server is not ready in 15 attempts (× 2s),
the job fails with a clear timeout message.
Deploy triggered before CI smoke tests passed — bad code deployed to Railway
design decision: needs: [] acknowledged
deploy.yml has needs: [] meaning it runs independently of ci.yml on the same push. If CI fails after deploy starts, broken code is already on Railway.
The post-deploy health check is the only safety net.
Accepted trade-off: adding needs: [test] would require both workflows in the same file. Kept separate for clarity.
The post-deploy health check catches most issues. Full sequential gating is a Phase 11 improvement.
CI smoke tests hit 429 — rate limiter blocked the test runner
fix: raise RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS to 1000 in CI env
The auth limiter (10 req/15min) was triggered by repeated register and login calls during CI. With the same IP address making all requests,
the limiter fired before all smoke tests could complete.
Fix: CI env sets RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS: 1000. This raises the global limiter ceiling only in the CI environment.
Production and local dev still enforce the real limits. Separately, CI uses a unique ci-test email to avoid auth limiter conflicts.
Container crashed on startup — Prisma client missing from final image
fix: explicit COPY --from=deps .prisma
prisma generate outputs the client to node_modules/.prisma. The COPY --from=deps node_modules copies node_modules but not the hidden
.prisma subdirectory in all environments.
Fix: added an explicit COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules/.prisma ./node_modules/.prisma line in the runner stage.
Both copy statements are required. Missing either one causes a runtime crash.
red = crash / build failure
yellow = pipeline logic risk
blue = container / runtime design
